description: Pale pink to white gums in a German Shepherd with bloated abdomen
notes: Pale gums + distended abdomen in a deep-chested breed = GDV/bloat until proven otherwise. This combination is a surgical emergency. Never recommend monitoring at home.

description: Bright red gums with petechiae (small red spots) in a Pit Bull
notes: Petechiae on gums suggest thrombocytopenia. Could be IMHA, tick-borne disease, or DIC. Must check platelet count. Do not assume gingivitis.
Icteric gums = hyperbilirubinemia. Could be liver failure, hemolytic anemia, or biliary obstruction. Labrador-specific: consider copper storage hepatopathy.

description: Blue/purple gums (cyanosis) in a French Bulldog
notes: Cyanotic gums = severe hypoxia. In brachycephalic breeds, could be BAOS crisis, pneumonia, or heart failure. This is an absolute emergency.

description: Healthy pink gums with normal capillary refill time in a Beagle
notes: Normal gum color. Capillary refill <2 seconds is normal. Must still correlate with other symptoms the owner reports.

description: Dry tacky gums in a Golden Retriever with vomiting and diarrhea
notes: Dry tacky gums = 5-7% dehydration. With vomiting + diarrhea, risk of rapid progression. Must assess skin tenting and check for bloody stool.
